Title: Search Contact with Wildcard Operator
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), multiple contacts exist with varied names
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Locate the search bar
3. Enter a search term using the asterisk wildcard (e.g. John*)
4. Press Enter or click Search
5. Verify the results include all contacts whose names start with the prefix
6. Try a mid-string wildcard (e.g. *son) and verify trailing match behavior
7. Verify results are limited to relevant matches only
Expected Result:
- Wildcard (*) operator is supported in the contact search field
- Search with prefix wildcard returns all matching contacts
- Mid-string wildcard returns contacts with the suffix in the name
- Non-matching wildcard queries return an empty result set
- Search results update without page reload

Title: Search Contact with Replace Operator
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), contacts exist that differ by a single character in their names
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Enter a search term using the question mark replace operator (e.g. Jo?n)
3. Press Enter or click Search
4. Verify results include contacts matching the single-character wildcard
5. Try ? at different positions in the query
6. Verify non-matching queries return empty results
Expected Result:
- Question mark (?) operator is supported in the contact search field
- Single-character wildcard matches exactly one character at its position
- Results include contacts like John, Joan matching Jo?n pattern
- Pattern matching is case-insensitive
- Empty result set is displayed when no contacts match the pattern

Title: Contact Filters UI

- Clearing filters restores the full unfiltered list
- Filter state is maintained during pagination

Title: Multi-Org Contact Hierarchy
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), two separate organizations exist, a contact is a member of one organization only
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Locate a contact assigned to Organization A
3. Verify the contact appears in Organization A member list
4. Navigate to Organization B
5. Verify the contact does not appear in Organization B member list
6. Add the contact to Organization B via the member management UI
7. Verify the contact now appears in both Organization A and B
8. Remove the contact from Organization A and verify they remain in Organization B only
Expected Result:
- Contact visibility is scoped to the organizations they belong to
- A contact not in Organization B is not visible in its member list
- Contact can be added to multiple organizations
- Removing a contact from one organization does not affect membership in others
- Organization hierarchy is correctly maintained for reporting and access control

Title: Invite customer from Contacts toolbar — full flow
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), at least one store configured, email service operational
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts
2. Locate and click 'Invite customers' button in the toolbar
3. Verify the Invite blade opens with fields: Store (dropdown), Language (dropdown), Organization (dropdown), Role (dropdown), Emails (text input), Message (textarea)
4. Select a Store from the dropdown (e.g. B2B-store)
5. Verify Language auto-populates from store default
6. Leave Organization empty
7. Verify Role defaults to 'Organization employee'
8. Enter a valid email address in the Emails field
9. Click the 'Invite' button
10. Verify success response in the UI (toast or confirmation message)
11. Check network request: POST /api/members/customers/invite with correct payload
Expected Result:
- Invite blade opens from Contacts toolbar with all expected fields
- Store dropdown lists all configured stores
- Language auto-populates from selected store default language
- Role defaults to 'Organization employee'
- POST /api/members/customers/invite is sent with storeId, emails, cultureName, role
- API returns HTTP 200 with succeeded:true
- UI displays success confirmation

Title: Invite form validation — required fields
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL})
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts > Click 'Invite customers'
2. Leave all fields empty > Verify 'Invite' button is disabled or clicking it shows validation errors
3. Select a Store but leave Emails empty > Verify Invite button remains disabled or shows email required error
4. Clear Store, enter an email address > Verify Invite button remains disabled or shows store required error
5. Select Store AND enter email > Verify Invite button becomes enabled
6. Clear the email field again > Verify button disables
7. Enter only whitespace in email field > Verify validation rejects it
Expected Result:
- Invite button is disabled when Store is empty
- Invite button is disabled when Emails field is empty
- Both Store and at least one email are required to enable submission
- Whitespace-only email input is rejected
- Validation messages are clear and visible

Title: Invite API — valid request
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), valid OAuth2 token obtained, store and organization exist
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/members/customers/invite with valid payload:
   { "storeId": "${STORE_ID}", "emails": ["testinvite@example.com"], "cultureName": "en-US", "roleId": "org-employee", "message": "Welcome" }
2. Verify HTTP 200 response
3. Verify response body contains succeeded:true
4. Verify response includes per-email result details
5. Send same request again with a different email to confirm repeatability
Expected Result:
- POST /api/members/customers/invite returns HTTP 200
- Response body: { succeeded: true } or equivalent success indicator
- Per-email results included in response
- Request is repeatable with different email addresses
- Content-Type is application/json

Title: Invite API — empty body
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), valid OAuth2 token obtained
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/members/customers/invite with empty body: {}
2. Verify response status code (expected: 200 with succeeded:false, or 400)
3. Verify response contains error code or validation message
4. Send POST with null body
5. Verify response contains error information
6. Send POST with body: { "emails": [] } (empty array)
7. Verify response indicates no emails provided
Expected Result:
- Empty body request does not cause HTTP 500
- Response contains succeeded:false with descriptive error code/message
- Null body is handled gracefully
- Empty emails array returns validation error
- No unhandled exceptions or stack traces in response
- Note: API currently returns HTTP 200 with succeeded:false for validation errors instead of HTTP 400

Title: Invite API — invalid email format
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), valid OAuth2 token obtained
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/members/customers/invite with payload: { "storeId": "${STORE_ID}", "emails": ["not-an-email"] }
2. Verify response indicates email validation failure
3. Send with emails: ["@missing-local.com"]
4. Verify validation failure
5. Send with emails: ["spaces in@email.com"]
6. Verify validation failure
7. Send with emails: ["valid@test.com", "invalid"] (mixed valid and invalid)
8. Verify per-email validation results
Expected Result:
- Malformed email addresses are rejected with clear error messages
- Various invalid formats are caught: missing @, missing domain, spaces, missing local part
- Mixed valid/invalid emails: valid ones may succeed while invalid ones fail (or all fail depending on implementation)
- No HTTP 500 errors for invalid input
- Error messages reference the specific invalid email

Title: Invite API — already registered email
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), valid OAuth2 token obtained, a user account already exists with known email
Steps:
1. Identify an existing registered user email from the system
2. Send POST /api/members/customers/invite with payload: { "storeId": "${STORE_ID}", "emails": ["existing-user@example.com"] }
3. Verify response indicates the email is already registered
4. Verify the response contains succeeded:false or a per-email error for the duplicate
5. Verify the existing user account is NOT modified or duplicated
6. Send invite with mix of new and existing emails
7. Verify per-email results correctly distinguish new vs existing
Expected Result:
- Already registered email returns an appropriate error (not a generic 500)
- Response clearly indicates the email is already in use
- Existing user account remains unchanged
- Mixed batch: new emails succeed, existing emails fail with clear per-email status
- No duplicate accounts created

Title: Invite RBAC — permission required
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), two test users: User_Admin with full customer permissions, User_Limited with only customer:read and customer:access (no customer:create)
Steps:
1. Login as User_Admin > Navigate to Contacts > Verify 'Invite customers' button is visible
2. Click 'Invite customers' > Verify blade opens and invite can be submitted
3. Logout > Login as User_Limited (customer:read + customer:access only)
4. Navigate to Contacts > Verify 'Invite customers' button is hidden or disabled
5. Attempt direct API call as User_Limited: POST /api/members/customers/invite
6. Verify API returns HTTP 403 Forbidden or 401 Unauthorized
7. Login as Admin > Grant customer:create to User_Limited role
8. Login as User_Limited > Verify 'Invite customers' button now appears
Expected Result:
- Users with full customer permissions see and can use the Invite button
- Users without customer:create permission do not see the Invite button
- Direct API call without permission returns 403 Forbidden
- Granting customer:create permission enables the Invite feature
- No permission bypass possible via direct API access

Title: Invite with all 3 roles
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), store configured, 3 roles available: Organization employee, Organization maintainer, Purchasing agent
Steps:
1. Navigate to Contacts > Click 'Invite customers'
2. Select Store, enter email_1@test.com
3. Select role 'Organization employee' from dropdown > Click Invite
4. Verify API request contains roleId for Organization employee
5. Repeat: enter email_2@test.com, select 'Organization maintainer' > Invite
6. Verify API request contains roleId for Organization maintainer
7. Repeat: enter email_3@test.com, select 'Purchasing agent' > Invite
8. Verify API request contains roleId for Purchasing agent
9. Verify all 3 invitations succeeded
Expected Result:
- Role dropdown shows exactly 3 options: Organization employee, Organization maintainer, Purchasing agent
- Each role selection sends the correct roleId in the API request payload
- All 3 roles can be used to successfully send invitations
- Default role is 'Organization employee' when no selection is made

Title: Invite multiple emails in single request
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), store configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to Contacts > Click 'Invite customers'
2. Select Store and Role
3. Enter 3 email addresses: user1@test.com, user2@test.com, user3@test.com
4. Click Invite
5. Monitor network request: verify all 3 emails appear in the emails array of the POST payload
6. Verify response contains per-email success/failure status
7. Enter 1 valid and 1 invalid email > Submit
8. Verify per-email results: valid succeeds, invalid fails
9. Test with duplicate emails in the same request
Expected Result:
- Multiple emails can be entered and submitted in a single invite request
- API request payload contains all entered emails in the emails array
- Response provides per-email success/failure status
- Mixed valid/invalid: each email processed independently
- Duplicate emails in same request are handled gracefully (deduplicated or error per duplicate)

Title: Invite from Store context — store pre-filled
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), at least one store exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Stores
2. Open a Store detail blade (e.g. B2B-store)
3. Locate and click 'Invite customers' button in the store toolbar
4. Verify the Invite blade opens with Store field pre-filled with the current store
5. Verify the Store field is read-only or pre-selected
6. Verify Language auto-populates from this store's default language
7. Enter an email address and click Invite
8. Verify API request storeId matches the pre-filled store
Expected Result:
- Invite blade opens from Store detail toolbar
- Store field is pre-filled with the current store name/ID
- Store field cannot be changed (pre-selected context)
- Language defaults correctly from the store configuration
- API request storeId matches the pre-filled store

Title: Invite roles API — GET available roles
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), valid OAuth2 token obtained
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/members/customers/invite/roles
2. Verify HTTP 200 response
3. Verify response is a JSON array with exactly 3 role objects
4. Verify each role has id and name fields
5. Verify role names: 'Organization employee', 'Organization maintainer', 'Purchasing agent'
6. Verify role IDs are non-empty strings
7. Send request without auth token > Verify 401 Unauthorized
Expected Result:
- GET /api/members/customers/invite/roles returns HTTP 200
- Response contains exactly 3 roles
- Each role object has id (string) and name (string) fields
- Role names match: Organization employee, Organization maintainer, Purchasing agent
- Unauthenticated request returns 401
- Response Content-Type is application/json

Title: GraphQL inviteUser mutation
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), GraphQL xAPI endpoint accessible at ${BACK_URL}/ui/graphiql, store and organization exist
Steps:
1. Open GraphiQL at ${BACK_URL}/ui/graphiql
2. Execute inviteUser mutation:
   mutation { inviteUser(command: { storeId: "${STORE_ID}", emails: ["gqltest@example.com"], cultureName: "en-US", roleId: "org-employee", organizationId: "test-org-id", message: "GraphQL invite test" }) { succeeded errors { code description } } }
3. Verify response: { data: { inviteUser: { succeeded: true } } }
4. Execute with invalid storeId > Verify succeeded:false with error
5. Execute with empty emails array > Verify error response
6. Verify inviteUser mutation appears in schema introspection with InputInviteUserType
Expected Result:
- inviteUser mutation executes successfully via GraphQL
- Response includes succeeded boolean and errors array
- Valid request returns succeeded:true
- Invalid storeId returns succeeded:false with descriptive error
- Empty emails returns error
- Mutation is discoverable via schema introspection

Title: Invite with custom message
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), store configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to Contacts > Click 'Invite customers'
2. Select Store and enter email
3. Locate the Message textarea field
4. Enter a custom message: 'Welcome to our B2B platform! Your account is ready.'
5. Click Invite
6. Monitor network request: verify message field is included in the POST payload
7. Verify API response is succeeded:true
8. Repeat without message (leave textarea empty) > Verify invite still succeeds
9. Enter a very long message (500+ characters) > Verify behavior
Expected Result:
- Message textarea is visible and editable on the Invite blade
- Custom message is included in the API request payload as 'message' field
- Invite succeeds with a custom message
- Invite succeeds without a message (field is optional)
- Long messages are either accepted or truncated with feedback to the user
- Message field does not break invite functionality

Title: Invite language selection
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), store configured with multiple languages
Steps:
1. Navigate to Contacts > Click 'Invite customers'
2. Select a Store > Verify Language dropdown auto-populates with store default language
3. Open Language dropdown > Verify available options (up to 14: EN, DE, FR, ES, NO, SV, PL, IT, PT, JA, ZH, FI, RU, and store default)
4. Select 'de-DE' (German)
5. Enter email and click Invite
6. Monitor API request: verify cultureName is 'de-DE'
7. Change store > Verify language resets to new store default
8. Select 'ja-JP' (Japanese) > Invite > Verify cultureName is 'ja-JP'
Expected Result:
- Language dropdown auto-populates from store default language
- Dropdown lists all configured languages for the selected store
- Selecting a different language updates cultureName in the API request
- Changing the store resets the language to the new store's default
- Non-default languages (de-DE, ja-JP, etc.) are correctly sent in the invite payload

Title: Invite notification template rendering
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), notification service operational, store configured
Steps:
1. Send an invite via POST /api/members/customers/invite with valid payload including organizationId
2. Navigate to More > Notifications > Notification activity feed (or check notification log)
3. Search for the most recent notification sent to the invited email
4. Verify notification type is 'Signup invite notification (with organization)' when org is specified
5. Verify template variables are replaced: invite URL is populated, organization name appears, custom message appears
6. Send another invite WITHOUT organizationId
7. Verify notification type is 'Signup invite notification (customer)' (without org template)
8. Verify invite URL in the notification is valid and clickable
Expected Result:
- Invitation triggers a notification in the system notification log
- With organization: uses 'Signup invite notification (with organization)' template
- Without organization: uses 'Signup invite notification (customer)' template
- Template variables (invite_url, organization name, message) are correctly replaced
- Invite URL is a valid, functional link
- Known issue: template body contains grammar error 'Please  to register by invite clicking here'

Title: Invite notification template content
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL})
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Notifications > Notification templates
2. Search for 'Signup invite notification (customer)' template
3. Open template > Verify subject line, body content, and template variables: {{ message }}, {{ invite_url }}
4. Search for 'Signup invite notification (with organization)' template
5. Open template > Verify it includes organization-specific variables in addition to base variables
6. Verify both templates have correct sender configuration
7. Note any grammar errors in template body text
Expected Result:
- Both invite notification templates exist in the Notifications module
- 'Signup invite notification (customer)' template contains {{ message }} and {{ invite_url }} variables
- 'Signup invite notification (with organization)' template contains organization-specific variables
- Templates have subject line and body configured
- Known issue: template body has grammar error 'Please  to register by invite clicking here' (double space and incorrect word order)

Title: Invite without organization — correct template used
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), store configured, notification log accessible
Steps:
1. Navigate to Contacts > Click 'Invite customers'
2. Select Store, enter email, leave Organization field empty
3. Click Invite > Verify success
4. Check notification log or API: verify the notification used 'Signup invite notification (customer)' template (NOT the 'with organization' template)
5. Now send another invite WITH an Organization selected
6. Verify notification used 'Signup invite notification (with organization)' template
7. Compare the two notification bodies to confirm different templates were applied
Expected Result:
- Invite without organization uses 'Signup invite notification (customer)' template
- Invite with organization uses 'Signup invite notification (with organization)' template
- Template selection is automatic based on whether organizationId is provided
- Both templates render correctly with appropriate content

Title: Invite store dropdown options
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), multiple stores configured in the system
Steps:
1. Navigate to Customer module > Contacts > Click 'Invite customers'
2. Click on the Store dropdown field
3. Verify all configured stores appear in the dropdown list
4. Verify store names are readable and correctly displayed
5. Select each store one by one > Verify Language updates accordingly
6. Navigate to More > Stores > Count the active stores
7. Compare active store count with dropdown options count
Expected Result:
- Store dropdown lists all active/configured stores in the system
- Store count in dropdown matches the number of active stores in Store management
- Each store name is displayed correctly (no truncation or encoding issues)
- Selecting different stores updates the Language dropdown to each store's default language
- No duplicate or inactive stores appear in the dropdown

Title: GraphQL inviteUser with customerOrderId
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), GraphQL xAPI endpoint accessible, an existing order ID available, store configured
Steps:
1. Open GraphiQL at ${BACK_URL}/ui/graphiql
2. Introspect InputInviteUserType > Verify customerOrderId field exists in the schema
3. Execute inviteUser mutation with customerOrderId:
   mutation { inviteUser(command: { storeId: "${STORE_ID}", emails: ["orderinvite@example.com"], cultureName: "en-US", customerOrderId: "existing-order-id" }) { succeeded errors { code description } } }
4. Verify response succeeded:true
5. Verify the invited user is associated with the specified order (check via order API or admin)
6. Execute with invalid customerOrderId > Verify error handling
7. Note: customerOrderId field is available in GraphQL but NOT exposed in the Admin UI Invite blade
Expected Result:
- InputInviteUserType schema includes customerOrderId field (string, optional)
- Mutation with valid customerOrderId executes successfully
- Invited user is linked to the specified order
- Invalid customerOrderId returns a descriptive error (not 500)
- Known limitation: customerOrderId is only available via GraphQL API, not in Admin UI
- This enables order-linked invitation workflows for B2B scenarios
